input_row.title: Ensure that Activity Log Alert exists for Delete Security Solution
input_row.description: Create an activity log alert for the Delete Security Solution event.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring for Delete Security Solution events gives insight into changes to the active security solutions and may reduce the time it takes to detect suspicious activity.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Navigate to the Monitor blade. 2. Click on Alerts. 3. In the Alerts window, click on Alert rules. 4. Ensure an alert rule exists where the Condition column contains Operation name=Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete. 5. Click on the Alert Name associated with the previous step. 6. Ensure the Condition panel displays the text Whenever the Activity Log has an event with Category='Administrative', Operation name='Delete Security Solutions' and does not filter on Level, Status or Caller. 7. Ensure the Actions panel displays an Action group is assigned to notify the appropriate personnel in your organization. Audit from Azure CLI az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription <subscription Id> --query "[].{Name:name,Enabled:enabled,Condition:condition.allOf,Actions:actions}" Look for Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete in the output Audit from PowerShell Get-AzActivityLogAlert -SubscriptionId <subscription ID>|where-object {$_.ConditionAllOf.Equal -match "Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete"}|select-object Location,Name,Enabled,ResourceGroupName,ConditionAllOf  Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: b954148f-4c11-4c38-8221-be76711e194a - Name: 'An activity log alert should exist for specific Administrative operations'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Navigate to the Monitor blade. 2. Select Alerts. 3. Select Create. 4. Select Alert rule. 5. Choose a subscription. 6. Select Apply. 7. Select the Condition tab. 8. Click See all signals. 9. Select Delete Security Solutions (Security Solutions). 10. Click Apply. 11. Select the Actions tab. 12. Click Select action groups to select an existing action group, or Create action group to create a new action group. 13. Follow the prompts to choose or create an action group. 14. Select the Details tab. 15. Select a Resource group, provide an Alert rule name and an optional Alert rule description. 16. Click Review + create. 17. Click Create. Remediate from Azure CLI az monitor activity-log alert create --resource-group "<resource group name>" --condition category=Administrative and operationName=Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete and level=<verbose | information | warning | error | critical> --scope "/subscriptions/<subscription ID>" --name "<activity log rule name>" -- subscription <subscription id> --action-group <action group ID>  Remediate from PowerShell Create the Conditions object. $conditions = @() $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Administrative -Field category $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete -Field operationName $conditions += New-AzActivityLogAlertAlertRuleAnyOfOrLeafConditionObject - Equal Verbose -Field level Retrieve the Action Group information and store in a variable, then create the Actions object. $actionGroup = Get-AzActionGroup -ResourceGroupName <resource group name> - Name <action group name> $actionObject = New-AzActivityLogAlertActionGroupObject -Id $actionGroup.Id Create the Scope object $scope = "/subscriptions/<subscription ID>" Create the Activity Log Alert Rule for Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete New-AzActivityLogAlert -Name "<activity log alert rule name>" - ResourceGroupName "<resource group name>" -Condition $conditions -Scope $scope -Location global -Action $actionObject -Subscription <subscription ID> -Enabled $true Default Value: By default, no monitoring alerts are created. References: 1. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/updates/classic-alerting-monitoring-retirement 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-in/azure/azure-monitor/platform/alerts-activity-log 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-in/rest/api/monitor/activitylogalerts/createorupdate 4. https://docs.microsoft.com/en- in/rest/api/monitor/activitylogalerts/listbysubscriptionid 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-logging-threat- detection#lt-3-enable-logging-for-security-investigation

first_opinion.response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit can be performed using Azure Resource Manager APIs and commands provided in the audit field. Specifically, the control requires checking for the existence of an activity log alert for the "Delete Security Solutions" event. Both Azure CLI and PowerShell commands are provided for this purpose, which suggests that the control can be fully automated using existing APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_monitor_activity_log_alert_delete_security_solution_exists

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

- **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
  - Azure CLI command: `az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription <subscription Id> --query "[]"`
  - PowerShell command: `Get-AzActivityLogAlert -SubscriptionId <subscription ID>`

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Subscriptions

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Ensure at least one alert exists where:
    - The condition includes an operation name: `Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete`
    - The action group is properly configured to notify appropriate personnel.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** At least one alert rule exists that meets the specified conditions.
  - **Fail:** No alert rule exists, or the existing alert rule does not meet the specified conditions.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - Example CLI command for audit check:
    ```bash
    az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription <subscription Id> --query "[?contains(condition.allOf, 'Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete')].{Name:name, Enabled:enabled, Condition:condition.allOf, Actions:actions}"
    ``` 

This checks all existing activity log alerts in the subscription to ensure compliance with the control regarding "Delete Security Solutions" events.

second_opinion.response:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The task involves checking the existence of an activity log alert, which can be efficiently performed using Azure Resource Manager APIs, Azure CLI, or PowerShell cmdlets. These tools allow for automated querying and validation of alert configurations, making manual checks unnecessary.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of Azure CLI and PowerShell commands to list and query activity log alerts is appropriate. The CLI command provided is correct in its structure and intent. However, the PowerShell command should include a filter to specifically check for the "Delete Security Solutions" event, similar to the CLI command's query.
- The program name follows the convention well, reflecting the service, resource, and security intent.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The PowerShell command could be improved by including a filter to ensure it checks for the specific operation name `Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete`. This would align it with the CLI command's functionality.
- Consider including a check to verify that the action group associated with the alert is correctly configured to notify the appropriate personnel, as this is part of the control's intent.
- It might be beneficial to provide a more detailed example of the expected output format for the CLI command to aid in understanding the pass/fail criteria.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- Given the use of Azure's native tools and APIs for this assessment, I have high confidence in the technical feasibility and correctness of the automated approach.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Enhance the PowerShell command to include filtering for the specific operation name `Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete`. Additionally, ensure that the action group configuration is part of the validation process to fully meet the control's requirements. This will ensure comprehensive compliance verification.

final_decision.response:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the task of verifying the existence of an activity log alert for the "Delete Security Solutions" event can be automated using Azure Resource Manager APIs, Azure CLI, or PowerShell. These tools provide the necessary capabilities to query and validate alert configurations without manual intervention. The automation approach is technically feasible and aligns with the capabilities of Azure's APIs.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
azure_monitor_activity_log_alert_delete_security_solution_exists

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Azure Resource/Service: Azure Monitor
- API/CLI Method: `az monitor activity-log alert list --subscription <subscription Id> --query "[?contains(condition.allOf[].field, 'Microsoft.Security/securitySolutions/delete')]"`
- Properties to Check: Ensure the query returns at least one alert with the specified condition.
- Pass Criteria: At least one alert exists with the condition for "Delete Security Solutions."
- Fail Criteria: No alerts exist with the specified condition, indicating non-compliance.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both agreed on the feasibility of automation and provided similar reasoning and technical details.
